form.label: Admin: Sample Data
form.description: Create or delete synthetic (synthetic="true") wearable data for testing/demo. Admin-only (Create needs can_collect; Clean up needs can_delete). Pick an Action, set the options, and Submit â€” you'll be asked to approve the risky command (collect/delete) once per run.

search.query: | inputlookup wearable_person_profile | table person_id person_name | sort person_id

input.label: Confirm create
input.choice: Yes â€” create synthetic events (I can delete them later via Clean up)

search.query: | inputlookup wearable_person_profile | table person_id person_name | sort person_id

input.label: Confirm delete
input.choice: Yes â€” permanently delete the matching synthetic events

div.style: border:2px solid #F4A422; background:rgba(244,164,34,0.12); border-radius:6px; padding:12px 14px; margin-bottom:14px; color:#F4A422; font-weight:600

div.div:
âš After you click Submit, Splunk shows a small "potential security risk" dialog (it approves the collect/delete command). On the dark theme it's easy to miss â€” look near the center of the screen and click "Run Query Anyway" to proceed. This is Splunk's built-in safeguard, expected for this tool.
Don't worry â€” nothing runs until you finish the pickers (including Confirm) and click Submit, so it's safe to look around. If you Cancel the dialog, that run is aborted (nothing was created or deleted) â€” just click Submit again and choose "Run Query Anyway."
div.p: Pick an Action above. Only the selected action's options and result appear, and each action runs only on Submit â€” so a Create submit can never trigger a delete, and vice versa.

ul.li: Create replays the shipped wearable_samples.csv (one authentic-shaped event of every Oura + Garmin sourcetype) into index=wearables via collect output_format=hec, so events pass through the real TA normalization. Stamped synthetic="true" + the chosen person_id, sprayed across the day range. Each Submit adds a batch (re-submitting doubles it; nightly dedup collapses exact copies). Needs can_collect. Requires the explicit Confirm create selection.

Clean up deletes synthetic events for the chosen person + vendor scope (All = everything). It runs across all time automatically (no time-picker step), so nothing is left behind. Needs can_delete. Requires the explicit Confirm delete selection.
Both are Splunk "risky commands" â€” after Submit, click the âš icon on the result panel to review + approve.

search.query: | inputlookup wearable_samples.csv | search $sample_vendor$ | eval day=mvrange(0,$sample_days$) | mvexpand day | eval _time=relative_time(now(),"-".day."d@d") + tonumber(offset_sec) | eval _raw=replace(_raw,"__PERSON__","$sample_person$") | eval _raw=replace(_raw,"__DAY__",strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d")) | fields _raw _time sourcetype | collect index=wearables output_format=hec | stats count as events by sourcetype | sort sourcetype

panel.title: Deleted synthetic: person=$cleanup_person$ Â· vendor=$cleanup_vendor$ (all time)

search.query: `widx` synthetic="true" person_id="$cleanup_person$" vendor="$cleanup_vendor$" | delete | stats sum(deleted) as records_deleted, sum(errors) as errors
